DOCUMENT:
Germany's grid operators reported record renewable output last month. According to a report from CleanEnergyWire [1], wind and solar covered 68% of national electricity demand on April 14th, a new national record. The report also notes that grid curtailment costs reached 412 million euros in the first quarter [1]. Some analysts believe this trend will continue for years, though such predictions are inherently speculative. A separate statement from the grid operator TenneT [2] confirmed that no blackouts occurred despite the surge.

DOCUMENT:
A new benchmark released by MLCommons [1] shows that inference costs for large language models fell by 40% year over year. The organization's report states that this is primarily driven by improved quantization techniques rather than new hardware [1]. Meanwhile, a blog post from a hobbyist [2] claims that ternary weight models can match full-precision accuracy, though this has not been independently verified. Industry watchers speculate that this could reshape edge deployment strategies.
